title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran hostilities have escalated into a full-scale conflict now on day 39 of US-Israeli airstrikes, with President Trump issuing an April 7 ultimatum threatening strikes on Iran's power plants, bridges, and water facilities unless Tehran reopens the Strait of Hormuz by 8 PM ET, after rejecting a 45-day ceasefire proposal. US Central Command conducted recent special operations deep inside Iran, including helicopter rescues of downed F-15E airmen amid enemy fire, marking the first verified ground insertions while hitting military targets on Kharg Island. No large-scale troop invasion has materialized despite regional deployments of marines and paratroopers, but tonight's deadline and stalled diplomacy heighten risks of further military action or de-escalation.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's trader consensus lead at 16% implied probability stems from their dominant UEFA qualifiers and recent 3-0 friendly win over Serbia, building on Euro 2024 success with young stars like Yamal driving fluid attacking play. France (13.5%) trails closely, bolstered by Mbappé's form despite Nations League hiccups, while England's 11.3% reflects Southgate's defensive solidity and Kane's scoring threat in a completed qualification cycle. Defending champions Argentina (9%) and Brazil (8.6%) lag after grinding CONMEBOL standings battles—Brazil scraping top six amid inconsistencies—highlighting South American parity. The 48-team expanded format, fresh group draw, and depth across UEFA powerhouses keep the race tight, with upsets viable in group stage and knockouts.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the clear early frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 26.4%, outpacing Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (8.5%) and Sen. Jon Ossoff (6.7%), despite national polling averages showing former Vice President Kamala Harris ahead around 27%. This divergence stems from Harris's baggage from the 2024 general election loss, contrasted with Newsom's recent commanding lead over her in a March 12 California primary poll and his aggressive national profile criticizing the Trump administration. Ocasio-Cortez draws progressive base support, while Ossoff appeals in battleground Georgia ahead of his 2026 reelection. The wide-open field lacks an incumbent, with consolidation likely hinging on 2026 midterm outcomes, early fundraising, key endorsements, and shifts in national polls.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran rejected a U.S.-backed two-stage ceasefire proposal mediated by Pakistan—the "Islamabad Accord"—which called for an immediate truce to reopen the Strait of Hormuz followed by 15-20 days of talks trading Iran's nuclear limits for sanctions relief. Tehran dismissed the temporary framework as unrealistic, demanding a permanent end to hostilities amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian petrochemical plants and infrastructure. President Trump's deadline for full strait access looms today at 8:00 PM ET, with threats of strikes on power plants and bridges if unmet, fueling escalation risks. Diplomatic efforts by Oman and Egypt continue, but faltering talks and recent rejections drive trader skepticism on near-term de-escalation.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability for the green jacket, buoyed by his world No. 1 ball-striking and steady 2026 results including a win at The American Express and bogey-free rounds at The Players, yet top contenders remain tightly bunched amid surging LIV Golf momentum from Jon Rahm and Bryson DeChambeau, who traded playoff finishes last week at LIV South Africa with DeChambeau's back-to-back triumphs. Rory McIlroy lurks at 6.5% chasing the career Grand Slam, while Xander Schauffele and Ludvig Aberg add depth via strong strokes gained tee-to-green metrics over the past three months. Collin Morikawa's lingering back injury introduces uncertainty despite his confirmation to play, and benign Augusta weather—sunny skies, low wind, minimal rain—sets up low-scoring potential favoring precise iron play and par-5 exploits across this elite, 90-plus-player field.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner, driven by his role as heir apparent in President Trump's administration and strong performances in late-March CPAC (53%) and JL Partners straw polls among Republicans, signaling robust party support amid high prediction market volume exceeding $350 million. California Governor Gavin Newsom follows closely, elevated by his national visibility as a Democratic Trump critic, recent book tour, and signals of post-2026 midterm entry. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's rise stems from donor backing and foreign policy wins like Venezuela operations. The fragmented field of over 30 contenders keeps probabilities tight two years pre-primaries, with 2026 midterms, endorsements, and scandals poised to create separation via turnout in swing states and Electoral College math.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 7?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' W17 dominance under 2026's new technical regulations has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus for the Drivers' Championship, with Russell's 43.5% implied probability reflecting his veteran consistency despite trailing Antonelli by nine points after three Grands Prix. Antonelli's back-to-back victories in China—ignoring a team order—and Japan, where a safety car aided his pole-to-win from Russell, who suffered battery issues finishing fourth, have surged his 30.8% odds amid his rookie sensation. Russell's Australian GP win and sprint success underscore Mercedes' 45-point constructors' lead, positioning them ahead of Ferrari's Leclerc (6.1%) and Hamilton (3.5%), while Red Bull's struggles sideline Verstappen (2.5%). Team principal Wolff's endorsement of intra-team racing without contact heightens the duel.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the frontrunner at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, driven by his Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative's appeal to populist voters skeptical of federal health agencies, bolstered by his high-profile role in the Trump administration and influence ahead of 2026 midterms. Vice President J.D. Vance follows at 38%, leveraging his position as heir apparent with strong MAGA backing and a recent poll win positioning him atop early straw polls. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 22% reflects momentum from reports of President Trump's donor surveys favoring him over Vance amid foreign policy successes. Late-March developments, including Vance's international trips and Trump's public comparisons, underscore a competitive early primary field shaped by administration insiders, with 2026 midterm outcomes as the next key catalyst.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 39.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions, bolstered by their league-best 62-16 record and clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed entering the final week of the 2025-26 regular season. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play and elite defense have fueled a dominant stretch, including a big win over the Lakers, positioning them for home-court advantage through the playoffs. San Antonio Spurs (15.2%) trail closely at 59-19 after an 11-game win streak snapped by Denver's 136-134 OT thriller on April 4, where Nikola Jokić's 40 points highlighted Nuggets' (9.0%) surge to 50-28. Boston Celtics (12.4%) hold strong at No. 2 East (53-25) despite Kristaps Porziņģis' knee uncertainty, while Detroit Pistons (5.6%) lead East standings at 57-22 but face playoff inexperience doubts versus OKC's proven title run.

title: Elon Musk # tweets March 31 - April 7, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs the 260-279 tweet range at 100% implied probability for Elon Musk's X posts from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7 12:00 PM ET, driven by real-time trackers logging exactly 268 posts as of early April 7 (2:20 AM ET sync), with a steady daily average of 38 amid his signature high-volume activity on topics like Tesla updates, AI developments, and platform announcements. This aligns with recent weeks' patterns—prior markets resolved near 200-220 for similar periods—reflecting Elon's consistent engagement as X's most influential user, amplified by viral moments and record platform usage spikes. An upset would require an unprecedented final-hours barrage exceeding 11 more posts before noon ET close, unlikely given historical trajectories and the brief window remaining.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices have surged above $115 per barrel as of April 7, propelled by escalating Middle East tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, injecting a substantial geopolitical risk premium into trader consensus despite bearish fundamentals. The EIA's latest report for the week ending March 27 showed a 5.5 million barrel inventory build to 461.6 million barrels and declining refinery inputs, while OPEC+ eight members initiated a gradual 1.65 million bpd output hike this month. US oil rig counts per Baker Hughes rose two to 411, signaling modest production uptick. Traders eye weekly EIA inventories on April 9 and IEA's April market report for catalysts that could extend the rally or trigger pullbacks amid the futures curve's backwardation.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors FC Bayern München at 40.5% implied probability for this UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first leg at the Santiago Bernabéu, driven by Harry Kane's confirmed recovery from an ankle injury and expected start up top, offsetting absences like Jamal Musiala and Alphonso Davies. Real Madrid, at 37.5%, faces challenges without Thibaut Courtois (thigh injury, Andriy Lunin deputizing) and Rodrygo (knee), with Ferland Mendy doubtful on a hamstring issue, though Federico Valverde returns from suspension to bolster midfield. Bayern's recent rampant form and stylistic edges temper Real's home advantage and unbeaten streak in nine head-to-heads (D2 L7 for Bayern), keeping the draw competitive at 23.5% in this high-stakes rivalry clash.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 8, 2026, parliamentary elections, official tallies with over 99% of precincts reporting confirm the Historic Pact for Colombia securing the most seats at 42 in the 183-member Chamber of Representatives, followed by Centro Democrático at 30, and Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) firmly in third with 26 seats—ahead of the Partido Conservador Colombiano's 19. This distribution, determined by the D'Hondt method across departmental circumscriptions, has solidified trader consensus at 96% implied probability for PLC in third place, reflecting the stability of results amid minor lingering escrutinio in areas like Bogotá. Potential challenges include rare legal disputes over seat allocations or recounts, though none have materially threatened PLC's lead as certification nears completion.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead trader consensus at 38.8% implied probability, driven by their dominant UMK 2026 win with "Liekinheitin"—a rock-classical-dance fusion that secured record public votes nearly triple the runner-up and now tops OGAE fan polls, streaming charts, and bookmakers. The duo's jury-televote balance positions them as a rare frontrunner akin to past dual victors. France's rising soprano Monroe follows at 10.4%, buoyed by her internally selected anthem "Regarde!" and direct Big Five final entry, generating buzz post-March release. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund sits at 10.3% after clinching Melodi Grand Prix with emotive Danish-language "Før vi går hjem." As semis approach May 12-14, Finland's qualification hurdle tempers enthusiasm amid volatile Eurovision dynamics.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 97.5% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, driven by resilient labor market data and persistent inflation pressures. March nonfarm payrolls surged 178,000—far exceeding estimates of around 60,000—while unemployment dipped to 4.3%, signaling no urgent need for cuts amid the Fed funds target range of 3.50%-3.75%. The March FOMC held rates steady, projecting just one 25 basis point reduction later in 2026, with Chair Powell noting anchored long-term inflation expectations despite geopolitical oil shocks. Challenges could arise from softer-than-expected April CPI on April 15 or weakening jobless claims, potentially tilting toward a cut, though upside risks from energy prices bolster the hold.

title: Oilers vs. Utah
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Oilers win, the market will resolve to "Oilers".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers face Utah Mammoth in a crucial late-season matchup at Delta Center, with both teams jockeying for playoff positioning—Oilers atop the Pacific Division at 39-29-9 and Mammoth fourth in the Central at 40-30-6. Oilers' trader sentiment hinges on mounting injury woes, including Zach Hyman sidelined with a lingering issue (potentially two weeks) and Leon Draisaitl out with a lower-body injury, not skating ahead of playoffs; Colton Dach returns from LTIR while Mattias Janmark remains season-ending. Utah holds home-ice edge starting a homestand, boasts stronger recent form (57.5% win rate last 40 games vs. Oilers' 52.5%), despite defensemen like MacKenzie Weegar (day-to-day) and others out. Oilers won their March 24 head-to-head but dropped 5-1 to Vegas Saturday, underscoring offensive vulnerabilities without stars.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Iran-Israel-US conflict ending by December 31, 2026 (85% implied probability), driven by sustained military exchanges now in their sixth week since US-Israeli airstrikes began on February 28 targeting Iran's nuclear sites, missile facilities, and leadership. Key recent catalysts include Israel's April 6 strike killing IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi, US-Israeli attacks on petrochemical hubs and northern infrastructure killing dozens, and Iran's missile salvos hitting Israeli cities like Haifa. Iran rejected a US temporary ceasefire on April 6 tied to reopening the Strait of Hormuz, prompting President Trump's April 7 deadline threats of escalated strikes on power plants. UN Security Council talks offer slim diplomatic hopes amid tit-for-tat risks.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Hungary's parliamentary election just five days away on April 12, trader consensus on Polymarket prices Péter Magyar's chances at 65.5% to succeed Viktor Orbán as prime minister, mirroring recent polling averages where Magyar's Tisza party leads Orbán's incumbent Fidesz by double digits among decided voters. A late-March Medián survey, known for its accuracy, showed Tisza at 58% versus Fidesz's 35%, a gap widened by economic discontent, youth mobilization for the challenger, and opposition unity behind the ex-insider Magyar. Orbán retains rural and older voter loyalty plus organizational edges in single-member districts, reinforced by U.S. Vice President JD Vance's April 7 Budapest visit decrying EU "interference." Polls remain volatile, with Fidesz's gerrymandered advantages potentially tipping a close national vote.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M at a slim 49.5% implied probability to win Colombia's May 31 presidential election first round or runoff, closely trailed by Historic Pact nominee Iván Cepeda Castro (44.5%) and Centro Democrático's Paloma Valencia (40.1%), reflecting a fragmented field after March 9 legislative elections where Petro's left secured a congressional plurality without a majority. Cepeda leads recent polls around 35% buoyed by primaries victory and resurgence in Petro's approval amid peace talks, but Valencia surges on security concerns and right-wing momentum, while Candidate M draws moderate voters wary of polarization. Key tipping factors include right consolidation, scandals like government intelligence controversies, or pre-election debates; a runoff looms without a 50% first-round winner.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Israel in March?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET(ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: The ongoing US-Israel-Iran war, ignited by joint US and Israeli strikes on Iranian military and nuclear sites starting February 28, 2026, drove trader consensus on Iran's retaliatory military actions. Iran launched multiple ballistic missile and drone barrages against Israel throughout March, including cluster munition attacks on Tel Aviv and other sites, culminating in strikes on March 31—directly resolving "Yes" for Israel in the market. In the past week, exchanges persisted with an Iranian missile hitting Ramat Hasharon on April 7 and Israel eliminating IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi; Iran rejected ceasefire proposals amid US warnings from President Trump. UN Security Council discussions continue, but escalation signals dominate amid degraded Iranian missile stocks.

title: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Cameron Norrie vs Alex de Minaur
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Alex de Minaur in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Cameron Norrie' if Cameron Norrie advances against Alex de Minaur.

This market will resolve to 'Alex de Minaur' if Alex de Minaur advances against Cameron Norrie.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Norrie vs. Minaur: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Alex de Minaur in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 8 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex de Minaur enters the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters second round as the seeded No. 6 contender with superior overall form (strong 2026 record) and enhanced clay proficiency, evidenced by his 2025 semifinals run here and rested status via first-round bye, positioning him as trader consensus favorite despite the slow, high-bouncing clay surface. Cameron Norrie, ranked No. 24, carries momentum from a gritty three-set R64 win over Miomir Kecmanovic (6-2, 4-6, 7-6(5)) to open his clay swing 1-0, plus a 3-2 head-to-head edge including his March 2026 Indian Wells victory over de Minaur. Potential fatigue from Norrie's recent marathon could factor, though no injury concerns affect either; stylistic matchup favors de Minaur's speed against Norrie's baseline grinding.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US precision strikes targeted military sites on Iran's Kharg Island overnight April 7, sparing critical oil facilities that handle 90% of Tehran's crude exports, amid President Trump's looming deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and halt hostilities. This follows a March 13 US bombing raid, after which Iranian state media affirmed full armed forces control and uninterrupted oil shipments. Amid heightened US-Iran tensions, including threats of blockade or seizure, the island stays under Iranian operational authority with no verified loss of control. Traders monitor for ground escalations, Iranian retaliation via mines or drones, or diplomatic de-escalation, as geopolitical pressures could swiftly alter the status quo before any resolution date.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva as a narrow first-round favorite at 41.5% implied probability for Brazil's October 4, 2026 presidential election, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro close behind at 37%, mirroring late March polls from Paraná Pesquisas, Gerp, and AtlasIntel that show Lula leading 38-46% to Flávio's 36-40% amid undecided voters over 10%. High government disapproval near 54% and mutual negative images above 50% sustain the deadlock, as Flávio gains from anti-incumbent sentiment and his father's endorsement, while Lula leverages incumbency despite age concerns. Runoff scenarios often tie or favor Flávio slightly. Separation could arise from economic data, party conventions by August, or scandals shifting undecideds in this polarized PT-PL contest.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Sporting CP (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Sporting CP" if Sporting CP win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP face Arsenal in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at Estádio José Alvalade, with the hosts severely hampered by key absences: winger Nuno Santos sidelined by a thigh injury for around three weeks and captain Morten Hjulmand suspended, eroding defensive width and midfield control against Arsenal's high-pressing style. Both could return for the April 15 second leg at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal navigate their own injury concerns post-Carabao Cup final loss to Manchester City, with Mikel Merino out (foot), Eberechi Eze doubtful (month sidelined), and Martin Ødegaard, Jurrien Timber, Leandro Trossard as question marks, though no fresh issues reported recently. Arsenal boast nine UCL wins from ten this season, while Sporting reach their first quarter-final amid strong Primeira Liga form but recent fixture rescheduling for extra preparation. Home advantage and aggregate scoring favor a cautious Sporting approach, but trader consensus likely reflects Arsenal's edge in squad depth and momentum.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 95.3% implied probability that the Iranian regime will fall by April 30, driven by US intelligence assessments as recent as mid-March confirming the leadership remains intact and not at imminent risk of collapse despite ongoing US-Israeli airstrikes since late February. Security forces have suppressed earlier 2025-2026 protests without mass defections, and wartime conditions have rallied regime support while degrading military capabilities without sparking uprisings. Historical resilience against internal dissent and external pressure underpins this high confidence, with the three-week timeline offering little room for sudden shifts. Realistic disruptors include a supreme leader health crisis, high-level coup, or total breakdown from escalated military action or failed ceasefire talks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kansas City Royals hold momentum after a 4-2 victory over the Cleveland Guardians in Monday's series opener at Progressive Field, powered by Michael Wacha's seven innings of one-run ball and homers from Carter Jensen and Jonathan India, improving their early AL Central mark to around 5-5 while Guardians sit at 6-4 atop the division. Tuesday's pitching matchup features Royals' Noah Cameron (1-0, 1.80 ERA) against Guardians' Gavin Williams (1-1, 2.25 ERA), both delivering strong early-season outings with solid command. Cleveland placed SS Gabriel Arias on the 10-day IL with a left hamstring strain from game one, recalling INF Juan Brito for his MLB debut, thinning infield depth; Royals' bullpen misses Carlos Estévez (foot, IL). Chilly Cleveland weather and home-field edge favor low-scoring affair in this tight divisional clash.

title: Indian Premier League: Rajasthan Royals vs Mumbai Indians
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Rajasthan Royals and Mumbai Indians scheduled for April 7 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Rajasthan Royals vs Mumbai Indians - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Rajasthan Royals and Mumbai Indians scheduled for 2026-04-07 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Rajasthan Royals vs Mumbai Indians - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Rajasthan Royals and Mumbai Indians scheduled for 2026-04-07 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rajasthan Royals enter unbeaten at 2-0 with a potent batting lineup led by explosive opener Vaibhav Suryavanshi and captain Riyan Parag, fresh off a high-scoring 210 against Gujarat Titans and a tense bowling defense, positioning them third on the IPL 2026 points table with +2.233 NRR. Mumbai Indians, sixth after a six-wicket loss to Delhi Capitals exposing bowling frailties, regain captain Hardik Pandya post-illness alongside Trent Boult, bolstering Jasprit Bumrah's powerplay threat at seam-friendly Barsapara Stadium in Guwahati, where early moisture favors pacers and average first innings totals hover around 143. MI won the toss to bowl first in a rain-reduced 11-over contest, heightening upset potential in this evenly matched head-to-head rivalry (MI leads 16-14), with Jofra Archer's death overs pivotal for RR.
